title: Meta decision threshold (0.5/0.55) mismatched to low TP base rate (~0.22) from 3R barriers
why: 3R asymmetric barriers make P(label=1)=TP-hit ~0.22, so the calibrated meta outputs probabilities centred at ~0.22 (OOF medians 0.21-0.24). The 0.5 decision threshold (spec headline 0.55) sits far above that mass, so frac(prob>0.5) is 0.10-0.17 (ema_cross) / 0.00-0.04 (momentum_zscore; lgbm never exceeds 0.38). The meta therefore rejects nearly everything in folds 1&2 -> 0 trades -> stack gate cannot pass. The threshold grid only sweeps upward (0.50-0.65), the wrong direction for a low-base-rate positive class.
value_rationale: Explains the persistent stack=False across the whole XAU D1 line; mis-diagnosed earlier as fold starvation. Gates whether the meta can ever be evaluated.
scope: Quantify base rate P(label=1) per fold and the prob-distribution vs threshold gap
Design a PRINCIPLED ex-ante threshold (e.g. relative to training-set base rate ~0.25-0.30) -- NOT a post-hoc test-grid pick (spec invariant defers per-fold inner-CV threshold to Paso 2)
CAVEAT: lowering threshold alone will not create alpha while meta MCC~=0 on ema_cross (hit~=base rate); it would add negative-EV trades. Pair with discrimination analysis.
Investigate the one live lead: momentum_zscore+xgb fold0 (hit 0.51, MCC 0.20, +2.14 Sharpe) -- why does xgb find signal there and does it generalise?
out_of_scope: Lowering min_trades_per_fold (load-bearing invariant) or changing n_folds as a workaround.

history.reason: threshold vs base-rate mismatch — corrected root cause of stack=False
chore(backlog): sync registry â€” positive-control + meta diagnosis (B0061-B0064)

Superseded/implemented by B0155 ev_breakeven_v1: audit threshold now derived from pre-registered barrier geometry (p*=(sl+C_ATR+lambda(tp+sl))/(tp+sl), p*=0.325 for tp=3/sl=1) instead of payoff-blind 0.50/0.55. Validated in batch_fable_v2: F002/F005/F006/F008 produced real falsifiable trade sets where fixed-0.50 produced NO_FIRE silence.
